must_detect.matched_text:
Strictly Private & Confidential — Inside Information under the UK Market Abuse Regulation — EMBARGO until 09:00 BST, 18 Oct 2026 — Project TIDAL GLASS Term Sheet.
Parties: Marleton Analytics plc (UK issuer) proposes to acquire a 72.5% stake in Seavale Sensors Ltd from Hearthfield Capital Partners LLP.
Consideration: £145,000,000 cash, funded by a £90,000,000 senior bridge facility from Cartwright & Devonshire Bank Ltd and a £55,000,000 equity placing.
Pricing: Equity placing at 312 pence per share, a 6.8% discount to the 5-day VWAP; admission intended to the Main Segment of the London Meridian Exchange subject to customary approvals.
Definitive Agreements: Share Purchase Agreement and Senior Facilities Agreement targeted for signature by 30 Sep 2026, with a long stop date of 30 Nov 2026.
Conditions Precedent: shareholder approval by simple majority, UK competition clearance, no Material Adverse Change in Seavale Sensors Ltd’s business between signing and completion.
MAC carve-outs: changes in UK base rates, industry-wide supply chain constraints, and loss of any single customer contributing <7% of revenue shall not constitute a MAC.
Non-public handling: this document contains inside information and must not be distributed beyond named recipients; board memo and term sheet to remain in IR-Red restricted folder.
Timetable: RNS-style announcement to be released immediately after embargo at 09:01 BST, 18 Oct 2026; target completion in Q4 2026; draft IR Q&A by 22 Sep 2026.
Governance: Marleton to nominate two directors to Seavale’s board post-completion; employment protections for transferring staff for 12 months consistent with UK practice.
Research and IR context: non-deal roadshow materials to reference public-source data from Marleton Analytics plc’s FY2025 annual report and The City Ledger’s June 2026 industry survey.
For the avoidance of doubt, background references to publicly available historical market size are non-confidential and excluded from the definition of inside information.
interim tasks: circulate draft spa and sfa tonight, update diligence tracker, move the slide deck by 3pm, arrange spa-day vouchers after signing.
Contacts: Ms. Eleanor Buckley, General Counsel, Marleton Analytics plc, eleanor.buckley@marleton.co.uk, +44 20 7940 8123; Mr. Felix Orr, Deal Lead, Hearthfield Capital Partners LLP, felix.orr@hearthfieldpartners.uk.
Compliance: Recipients are reminded of restrictions on unlawful disclosure, insider dealing, and market sounding under the UK Market Abuse Regulation and the issuer’s internal dealing code.
Forward-looking context: post-completion synergy run-rate expected at approximately £11,500,000 by FY2028 without constituting earnings guidance; pro forma net debt to EBITDA expected at ~2.4x.
